description: DEFERRED v2 design track (REVISED 2026-07-10, Holochain-first): make sovereign AZOA node operators members of a P2P network COMMUNITY without surrendering custody/private-data/autonomy. The federation substrate is a community-maintained Holochain hApp whose DHT IS the public ledger (facts, references, certification), NOT ActivityPub HTTPS documents and NOT blockchain consensus; HTTPS survives only as bootstrap/fallback transport. Sovereignty invariant: the shared DHT holds ONLY opted-in public facts (holon references, node descriptors, proof-of-certification) -- never an operator's private SurrealDB, custody keys, or unfederated holons -- so a shared fact ledger is not a shared database. Federation lives in a SEPARATE dedicated repo, likely AZOA.Commons (owns the DNA/hApp, runtime, and Holochain client); the AZOA node takes only a THIN client (AZOA.Commons.Client). Thin operator surface = 3 core functions: submit holons for federation, submit audit/conformance data to stay certified, host a hApp cell (embedded or separate service). L0-L3 survive as capabilities reframed onto the substrate: L0 node identity + machine-readable G-gate conformance manifest (alpha-cheap local half, land early); L1 verifiable cross-node holon references (azoa:// resolve+verify, no private-DB replication); L2 DHT-native discovery (bootstrap peers first); L3 self-sovereign avatar portability across nodes with per-node revocable consent. Value is NOT federated (chain settles between nodes). Prerequisites: gate-result serializer (no conformance-manifest source today), versioned HolonType vocabulary (unversioned/fail-open today), node/agent identity binding, and a Holochain .NET client choice (roll-our-own vs Rust binding vs supervised conductor; vendored NextGenSoftwareUK client is prior-art-only). Activation-gated on build-side traction (>=3 operators + >=1 real cross-node use case).
